commentary: 0.1 Mukesh to B Sai Sudharsan, FOUR runsNo swing, too straight, clipped away. Length ball on the pads, flicked late, as the ball approached the top of the pad
0.2 Mukesh to B Sai Sudharsan,  1 runA bit of width, carved. The length ball just outside off goes to deep third
0.3 Mukesh to Gill, FOUR runsDeep point in place but has no chance. Races well to his right. Gill stays back and gently cuts it, uses his wrist and steers it with an almost vertical bat.
0.4 Mukesh to Gill,  1 runBack of length on off, defended off the front foot to cover point
0.5 Mukesh to B Sai Sudharsan,  1 runAnother flick, stopped by Nissanka at midwicket. Dive to the left, gets his body over it. Length ball on the pads again, shot similar to the first ball
0.6 Mukesh to Gill, no runLooks to turn it to midwicket, but ball hits bat hard and rolls back to the bowler. Back of length on leg, Gill had to check his shot before closing the bat face

1.1 Ngidi to B Sai Sudharsan, FOUR runsPunched through cover, so well timed. Sudharsan very still as ball makes contact, high elbows, shape-holding all check. Miller dives to the left, on the practice pitch, and may have grazed his palm. That'll hurt.
1.2 Ngidi to B Sai Sudharsan,  1 runPitched up on leg, flicked to deep midwicket
1.3 Ngidi to Gill, no runA bit wide on a back of length, stirs a cut to cover
1.4 Ngidi to Gill, no runGill sent back, no trouble. Ran halfway back but mid-off had to run a bit too. Pitched up at the stumps, Gill half-flicking past the bowler
1.5 Ngidi to Gill,  1 runLength at the stumps, flicked to deep square leg. 129ks
1.6 Ngidi to B Sai Sudharsan,  1 runLength on middle and off, may have been slow. Yep, 115ks. Flicked towards midwicket

2.1 Mukesh to B Sai Sudharsan, OUTChopped on, Mukesh points straight and leaps. 133ks seam-up length ball moves in off the pitch, causes Sudharsan to get an under-edge as he looks for a cross-bat square drive. Slices back onto the stumps, the bent elbows show how he's cramped for room. Orthodox batt
2.1 Mukesh to B Sai Sudharsan, 1 wideA bit of deviation down leg, goes past the attempted pull. Shortish ball at 133ks
2.2 Mukesh to Buttler, no runLength on the pads, flicked to midwicket
2.3 Mukesh to Buttler, no runLength on middle, flicked off the front foot to midwicket. 134ks
2.4 Mukesh to Buttler, no runWalks into a cover drive, finds the infielder. Got close enough to the pitch there
2.5 Mukesh to Buttler,  SIX runsTonks it! And this is vintage Buttler. Walks down, plants his front foot forward before swinging at the full ball coming into him. Through the line, over long-on
2.6 Mukesh to Buttler,  1 runLength around off, punched to mid-off for a quick single

2.6 Mukesh to Buttler, 1 wideMay have hit Rahul's finger, he throws his glove away. That swung after it passed the batter. It hit the fingertips, around the middle finger. He'll get medical assistance. Length ball down leg, Buttler came down and missed a leg glance

3.1 Axar Patel to Buttler, FOUR runsGreeted with... is that a four or a six? Fielder signals four. It's yorked the cushion. Lot of width, a bit too full, Buttler extends his arms and drives it flat over cover
3.2 Axar Patel to Buttler,  SIX runsLooped up, larruped away! Slides his front food leg side, then clubs it golf-style. Hit from well in front of the body. Clears wide long-on with ease
3.3 Axar Patel to Buttler, no runFlat length ball on middle, chopped to cover
3.4 Axar Patel to Buttler,  1 runPitched up at the stumps, driven to long-off
3.5 Axar Patel to Gill,  1 runPitched up around off, driven to long-off
3.6 Axar Patel to Buttler,  1 runArm ball on a length at off, punched without weight-transfer, to long-off

4.1 Mukesh to Buttler,  SIX runsOh wow, that is as relaxed as a side-walk scoop can be. Widens his stance, with both feet in the crease, as the bowler delivers the 134ks back of length ball, and lifts the bat as it makes contact with ball

4.2 Mukesh to Buttler,  SIX runsWhacks the top tier! 96 metres! Sounded like a monster too. Buttler got a slot ball at the stumps, so he got a fast down-swing and muscled it through. Over straight long-on
4.3 Mukesh to Buttler, FOUR runsGets followed, still flicks it away through midwicket. Clips the length ball from the top of the pads. Buttler on a roll
4.4 Mukesh to Buttler, no runHe misses for a change. Makes room and looks to carve a short, wide seam-up ball over middle stump. Was arching back as he played it, ball went over bat.

4.5 Mukesh to Buttler,  SIX runsThis time he opens up the off side. With panache too! Was loading up and then danced down while making room, got a length ball with a bit of width. Created the pace on it by freeing his arms and slapping it over cover
4.6 Mukesh to Buttler,  1 runShort around the helmet, Buttler gets the bat from high to low as he pulls it to deep square leg
5.1 Ngidi to Buttler, no runSees the slower one early, bashes it too but straight to mid-off. Slight fumble but it stays close. Pitched up, driven off the front foot
5.2 Ngidi to Buttler, no runLength just outside off, punched to cover
5.3 Ngidi to Buttler, FOUR runsSwat-pulled through midwicket. Dipping low full toss, he had to crouch on impact as he picked it from outside off
5.4 Ngidi to Buttler, no runSlower yorker just outside off, Buttler looks to drive through cover. Gets an inside edge onto pad and it pops forward
5.5 Ngidi to Buttler, no runPace back on. 123ks but looked quicker. Buttler flicks the length ball to mid-on, who's close. Wanted a run, Gill wisely never took off
5.6 Ngidi to Buttler,  1 runButtler keeps strike. Low, wide full toss, Buttler leans and clinks it to mid-off. DC take the timeout as soon as it's available
6.1 Axar Patel to Buttler,  1 runLength just outside off, punched off the back foot to deep cover.
6.2 Axar Patel to Gill,  1 runWide on a length, cut to deep point

6.3 Axar Patel to Buttler,  2 runsLength on leg, flicked to the right of deep midwicket. Fielder has to run in which allows two. Fifty up for Buttler in 24 balls, five sixes already Previous NextPhotos
6.4 Axar Patel to Buttler,  1 runLength on leg, flicked to deep midwicket
6.5 Axar Patel to Gill, FOUR runsInside out over cover. More square than straight. Opens the face and hits against the angle of the length ball that came in
6.6 Axar Patel to Gill, no runLength on middle, defended to the bowler
7.1 Kuldeep to Buttler,  1 runLength on leg, looks to flick it leg side. Leading edge goes into the ground and forward
7.2 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runGets away with a shortish ball outside off, pulled to long-on

7.3 Kuldeep to Buttler, OUTKnocked over, Kuldeep gets Buttler off his third ball. Was a long-hop, but at 95ks after bowling 89ks last two balls, and it didn't bounce. Kept low, lost a lot of pace off the pitch and beat the pull as Buttler got low and tried to adjust. Bottom hand came off as the bail
7.4 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runGoes back and flicks the length ball to long-on. 84ks
7.5 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runTwo called, long-off keeps it to one. Stubbs chasing it to the left. Wide and full, smothered drive along the ground
7.6 Kuldeep to Sundar, FOUR runsStubbs beaten this time. TO the right, slid away but Washington's straight drive was quick across the outfield. Just a touch too full from Kuldeep
8.1 Natarajan to Gill, no runLength just outside off, punched to cover
8.2 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runLength outside off, 131ks, punches it with an open face to deep point
8.3 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runWide on a length, punch-slapped to deep cover
8.4 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runStruck well, but long-off stops it. Overpitched slow ball just outside off, driven hard. Timed well, full follow-through and along the ground
8.5 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runLength on leg, flicked to deep midwicket
8.6 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runShort-arm cut, a shot almost unique to Gill. Rises, has the back-lift and contact of a cut but with no follow-through. Hit from shoulder-height to deep point
9.1 Kuldeep to Gill,  SIX runsKuldeep misses his line, Gill whacks it behind square. His neck's alright. The sweep was what he played when he sprained it during the SA Test too
9.2 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runLength on leg, flicked to deep midwicket
9.3 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runLength on off, flicked through square leg
9.4 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runLength just outside off, 91ks, skids through as Gill looks to cut it. Has to adjust to the lack of room, gets it through point
9.5 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runLength on leg, flicked to deep midwicket. GT's hundred is up
9.6 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runFuller side of length just outside off, bunted to deep cover
10.1 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runskips down, and swats what turns out to be a high-ish full toss around off to long-on

10.2 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runthe man at point tumbles to his left to keep this to a single, although he doesn't collect it cleanly. Slower ball from Natarajan, who goes on a length and wide of off. Cut away by Washy
10.3 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runshort delivery shaping away to finish around sixth stump. Swat-pulled to deep midwicket

10.4 Natarajan to Sundar, FOUR runspulled over short fine leg. Washy swivels to pull at this short ball rising towards his throat at middle, but gets inside the line of the ball to go over the fielder
10.5 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runa low full toss on middle and leg. Whipped to deep square leg
10.6 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runa yorker-length delivery angling across to arrive on middle. Clipped to deep midwicket
11.1 Nigam to Gill,  1 runone bounce to the man at deep midwicket. Nigam bowls short and quick, and on the stumps, and Gill goes back to pull
11.2 Nigam to Sundar, FOUR runsthis short ball goes for four, though. Washy goes back and opens his stance up at this short ball arriving around sixth stump, and pulls hard and wide of long-on

11.3 Nigam to Sundar,  SIX runssix over deep midwicket!! The change of angle doesn't matter. Short in length yet again, and this one angles into off or thereabouts. Washy clobbers the ball for a six deep into the stands

11.3 Nigam to Sundar, (no ball) FOUR runsanother short ball, another four. This beats the man at long-on as well, as Washy pulls this ball from outside off in a near carbon-copy way as the last ball. And, and... it's a no-ball too.
11.4 Nigam to Sundar,  1 runmuch fuller and slower this time. This dips on a full length on off, and Washy pushes it to wide long-on

11.5 Nigam to Gill,  SIX runsGill joins the party. Nigam bowls a low full toss on the stumps, and Gill, who has his front leg out of the way, thumps it back over the bowler's head to ping the sightscreen
11.6 Nigam to Gill, no runnicely bowled. Pretty slow, and on a fullish length just outside off. Gill has a jab but is beaten on the outside edge
12.1 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runfull and slowish on off. Pushed to long-on as the ball hints to turn away
12.2 Kuldeep to Gill, no runshort of a length, and angling across to arrive wide of off. Gill slashes away from his body to point

12.3 Kuldeep to Gill,  SIX runsbam!! Six over square leg. Gill looks extremely confident with the sweep today. Kuldeep bowls on a length, and around middle and leg. Gill clubs it away after clearing his front leg
12.4 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runthe wrong 'un. Shorter side of a length on leg, and it turns away. Gill punches from off to long-on
12.5 Kuldeep to Sundar, no runKuldeep tries something different. He goes wide of the crease, and fires this on a fullish length wide of off. Cut away to point
12.6 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runfull and straight, and nudged to deep midwicket. "Nice Kuli," says Rahul
13.1 Axar Patel to Sundar,  1 runon a fullish length, and angling across to arrive on off. Punched to long-off

13.2 Axar Patel to Gill,  1 runnearly taken!! Miller at long-on did very well to run and dive forward, and the ball just dropped off his hands. Gill jumped out and lofted Axar off a length ball, but it went off the inside half of the bat
13.3 Axar Patel to Sundar,  1 runon the bounce to fine leg. Washy sweeps this full toss just outside leg, and misses out on a four
13.4 Axar Patel to Gill,  1 runfull and on off, but slower. Driven to deep cover point

13.5 Axar Patel to Sundar, FOUR runspulled wide of long-on. Stubbs runs and dives to his left, but can't cut it off. Short ball from Axar, whose angle from around the wicket is taking it away from Washy. But the latter pulls from outside off
13.6 Axar Patel to Sundar,  1 runshort in length again, and outside off. Slapped to deep cover. The call was for two, but the pick up and throw was quick
13.6 Axar Patel to Sundar, 1 widebeaten... but it's called wide. Slower from Axar, who goes full and wide of off. Washy looks to sweep but misses
14.1 Ngidi to Sundar, no runWashy is beaten. Short of a length, and pitching on off. The ball shapes away, and Washy looks to slash but the ball goes over his bat
14.2 Ngidi to Sundar,  1 runhere comes the slower ball. At just 112kph. Ngidi goes on a length on leg, and Washy ends up tamely fending it towards mid-off
14.3 Ngidi to Gill,  1 runbottom edge, but it goes to the left of the man at short fine leg. Gill advances and has a slash, but the ball dips on to him and arrives as a low full toss just outside off
14.4 Ngidi to Sundar,  1 runthe dipping slower ball again. This one comes at 111kph. It is nearly a yorker on middle, and Washy pushes it to midwicket

14.5 Ngidi to Gill, FOUR runsswept away to deep square leg. That is Gill's fifty. This ball dips on to him on middle, and meets the bat as a dipping full toss. But Gill reads the lack of pace, and swings at it in the gap Previous NextPhotos
14.5 Ngidi to Gill, 1 widewide of off. Another slower delivery, but Ngidi bowls this from wide of the crease, and ends up going on a fullish length wide of off. Gill lets it go
14.6 Ngidi to Gill,  1 runNgidi keeps going at 110kph, and thereabouts. This is another low, dipping full toss, but around sixth stump. Swiped to deep midwicket
15.1 Natarajan to Gill,  1 runbowls a near yorker. Good start. Gill gets across and whips to deep midwicket
15.2 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runa full toss swinging in to arrive low on off. Driven down to long-off

15.3 Natarajan to Gill, FOUR runsbetween long-on and deep midwicket. Short ball from Natarajan on leg, and the ball seams away to finish on off. It was a slower ball too, but Gill read it and dispatched it with a pull

15.4 Natarajan to Gill, no runwide outside off. Another short delivery, but this one bounces more and ends further of off. Gill looks to ramp but misses. But wait, Axar has reviewed. Natarajan bowled an offcutter, with Gill standing a slight step towards off. The ball leaps and seams away, and is sh
15.5 Natarajan to Gill,  1 rungood delivery. This time Natarajan moves back to the yorker. Gill drags it from off to long-on
15.6 Natarajan to Sundar, no runvery full, and around fifth stump. Washy drills it, but finds the man at extra cover
16.1 Kuldeep to Gill,  SIX runsswung away over short fine leg!! Kuldeep bowls short and outside leg, and Gill swivels to ram it back behind the wicket with a pull
16.2 Kuldeep to Gill,  1 runnow he goes short and wide of off. Gill reaches out, and swats to long-on
16.3 Kuldeep to Sundar,  1 runbowls thus on a length, and wide of off. The ball turns away further, and Washy follows. Cut away to point in the end
16.4 Kuldeep to Gill,  SIX runsslower, fuller... but six still!! Gill waits for this full and slow ball dip on middle and off, and crashes it back down the ground

16.5 Kuldeep to Gill, no runohh, wide not given. Kuldeep bowls on a length, and wide-ish of off. Left alone by Gill, who reviews. Kuldeep pitched it around seventh stump, and replays show the ball being just inside the wide line. So it's not a wide
16.6 Kuldeep to Gill, no rungood work by Kuldeep. He moves and tumbles to his left to keep this to a dot. On a length, and wide of off, as Gill swatted it back to him
17.1 Ngidi to Sundar,  1 runstarts with a slower ball. On a length on off, and punched wide of long-off
17.2 Ngidi to Gill, no rungood ball. This is wide of off, and lands as a deep yorker. Gill looks to slash but misses

17.3 Ngidi to Gill, OUTtaken at short mid-off!! Another one at a lack of pace, and Gill is gone. He went lofting at this full delivery outside off, but seemed to be beaten by the slower ball. Gill opened the face of the bat to try and slice it - perhaps - but ended up skying the ball to where a shor
17.4 Ngidi to Phillips, no runNgidi welcomes Phillips with a wide yorker. It's a slower ball as well. Phillips reaches out at this dipping ball but misses
17.5 Ngidi to Phillips, no runon a fullish length around off, and Phillips can only push it back to Ngidi off an awkward length
17.5 Ngidi to Phillips, 1 widethis is a wide. Phillips lets it go as Ngidi bowls very full and wide of off
17.6 Ngidi to Phillips,  1 runthis dips on to Phillips, and lands as a wide-ish full toss outside off. Driven back to long-on's left

18.1 Natarajan to Phillips,  1 runon the bounce to Stubbs at long-on. It was a low full toss angling across, and Phillips lofted it from middle and off. Phillips gave up after hitting, assuming he was gone, and only ran after he realised he wouldn't be
18.2 Natarajan to Sundar, no runtries the scoop, but hits it to short fine leg. Washy gets across early, and Natarajan bowls a lowish full toss wide of off
18.3 Natarajan to Sundar,  1 runlovely bowling. Natarajan lands this yorker on off, and Washy, almost as a late resort, ends up dabbing it to short third
18.4 Natarajan to Phillips,  1 runanother attempted yorker. This lands as a low full toss just outside off. Driven to long-on

18.5 Natarajan to Sundar,  SIX runsbashed over square leg!! Washy nearly falls on his back, but gets to his first IPL fifty. He's deep in the crease, and swipes at this full toss around off. Previous NextPhotos

18.6 Natarajan to Sundar, FOUR runsthree fielders dive, but none can stop the ball. First Natarajan, then Stubbs, and then Miller. This is a full ball on off, and Washy drove it back to first beat the bowler, then mid-off, and then long-on running and sliding to his right
19.1 Mukesh to Phillips,  SIX runsover midwicket. A short-arm pull from Phillips. The ball is on a short of a length on the stumps, and Phillips uses his bottom hand to dump the ball
19.2 Mukesh to Phillips,  1 rungood comeback by Mukesh. He hits back with the yorker just outside off, and Phillips can only bunt this to extra cover

19.3 Mukesh to Sundar, OUTgot him!! Washy falls rather tamely after a very good knock. Mukesh angled this ball in from around the wicket, and bowled a low full toss. Washy opened the face of the bat at that, but only ended up popping it for extra coverWashington Sundar c Rana b Mukesh Kumar 55 (32b

19.4 Mukesh to Tewatia,  1 rundirect hit would have had Tewatia!! Axar was at mid-off, and missed despite Tewatia giving up. Mukesh bowled another yorker around off, and Tewatia pushed it back even as Phillips was more than halfway down just as ball hit bat

19.5 Mukesh to Phillips, no runlooks to reverse scoop, but misses. Then they think of a single, before Tewatia is sent back. The ball was full and wide-ish of off, as Phillips looked to go behind the wicket but missed

19.6 Mukesh to Phillips, FOUR runsno, it's s four!! That makes it 12 of the over, and GT get to 210. Mukesh bowls this full and wide of off, and Phillips lofts it by reaching out to go inside out over extra over
